cases.description: TRUE is a logical value -> TRUE
FALSE is a logical value -> TRUE
A plain number is not a logical value -> FALSE
cases.expected_note: Even though 1 and TRUE are arithmetically interchangeable, ISLOGICAL distinguishes the underlying boolean type from a number.

cases.description: Text is not a logical value -> FALSE
cases.expected_note: The text string "TRUE" is text, not the boolean TRUE.
